Use PascalCase for public and protected fields, properties, and constants (e.g., CustomerName, MaxRetries).
Use camelCase with a leading underscore for private fields (e.g., _customerName, _orderTotal).
Use camelCase for local variables and method parameters (e.g., itemCount, userName).
Always use Option Explicit On and Option Strict On at the project or file level to enforce explicit declarations and strict type checking.
Declare variables with explicit types rather than relying on type inference when the type is not obvious (e.g., Dim count As Integer = 0).
Use meaningful, descriptive names that reveal intent (e.g., invoiceTotal instead of x or temp).
Use Const for values known at compile time and Shared ReadOnly for values determined at runtime.
Prefer String.Empty over empty string literals "" for clarity.
Scope variables as narrowly as possible; declare them closest to their first use.
Use nullable value types (e.g., Dim result As Integer? = Nothing) when a value may legitimately be absent.
Use Enum types for related sets of named constants instead of magic numbers.
Use properties with backing fields instead of public fields for encapsulation in classes.
